title: Riot Platforms Reports 34% Revenue Surge in 2024 Driven by Mining Growth
text: Riot Platforms reported record full-year 2024 revenue of $376.7 million, representing a 34% increase from prior year. The company achieved net income of $109.4 million, turning profitable compared to a $49.5 million loss in 2023. Bitcoin mining revenue surged 70% to $321 million, driven by higher Bitcoin prices despite lower production due to halving event. Gross profit climbed to $147.6 million from $97.6 million, while adjusted EBITDA more than doubled to $463.2 million. The company strategically accumulated Bitcoin, ending the year with 17,722 BTC, a 141% increase from 2023. Bitcoin production declined 27% to 4,828 BTC due to the April 2024 halving event that reduced block rewards. Mining costs rose to $42,011 per Bitcoin in Q4, driven by elevated power expenses. The company deployed 154% growth in self-mining hash rate, significantly outpacing the global network growth of 67%. Engineering business generated $38.5 million in revenue while the E4A Solutions acquisition contributed $28.2 million in 2024. The company expressed confidence in achieving approximately 22% hash rate expansion in 2025.
